privileged_info_summary: The usa.gdt dataset is a real (non-synthetic) quarterly macroeconomic time series from 1984 Q1 to 2009 Q3 containing 103 observations. It includes two variables: Real Gross Domestic Product (GDP) from Federal Reserve FRED series GDPC96 and inflation rate from FRED series CPIAUCSL (Consumer Price Index). The dataset is part of the Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim) educational materials. The task requires creating GDP growth rates, running OLS regression, computing Durbin-Watson statistics, and applying Cochrane-Orcutt AR(1) correction. The metadata contains one factual error: GDP units are described as 'chained 2005 dollars' but GDPC96 is actually in 'Chained 2009 Dollars' per official FRED documentation.
